title: Cracked Heels No More: Discover 10 Powerful Herbal Teas
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cracked heels are a natural remedy that helps to soothe, moisturize, and heal dry, cracked skin on the heels. These teas contain beneficial properties that promote skin health and reduce inflammation. The best herbal teas for cracked heels include Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Urtica dioica, Taraxacum officinale, Avena sativa, and Cymbopogon citratus. If left untreated, cracked heels can cause discomfort, pain, and difficulty walking, negatively impacting daily activities and overall quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a natural remedy for cracked heels. The tea is made from the flowers of the Calendula plant, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These compounds help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with cracked heels. The antiseptic properties of the tea also help to prevent infection, making it an effective treatment for cracked heels. Additionally, the tea can help to promote healing and tissue repair, allowing the skin to regenerate and become healthy again. When applied topically, the tea can help to lock in moisture and reduce dryness, making it easier to manage cracked heels. This natural remedy is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for sensitive skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for cracked heels because it helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it easier to repair and soften dry, cracked skin on the heels.
Tannins help to heal cracked heels because they have antifungal and antibacterial properties that prevent infection and promote the growth of healthy skin tissue.
Saponins are good for cracked heels because they help to moisturize and soften the skin, reducing dryness and brittleness, while also protecting it from further damage.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for cracked heels
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea for cracked heels, start by gathering dried marshmallow roots (Althaea officinalis), which can be found in health food stores or online. Begin by measuring one teaspoon of the dried roots for every 8 ounces of water you plan to use. Then, boil fresh water in a pot and let it cool for a minute. Next, add the measured marshmallow roots to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots, and discard the solids. Your Althaea officinalis tea is now ready to use. To apply it to your cracked heels, soak your feet in a foot bath filled with the warm tea for 15-20 minutes, or apply the cooled tea directly to the affected areas using a cotton pad. This herbal tea is known to help soothe and moisturize dry skin, promoting healthy healing and reducing the appearance of cracked heels.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for cracked heels because it helps to soften and moisturize the skin. The tea contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other nutrients that help to repair damaged skin cells. When applied to cracked heels, the tea can help to reduce dryness and inflammation, promoting the growth of new skin cells. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it less painful to walk and move around. By regularly soaking your feet in Aloe barbadensis tea, you can help to hydrate and repair your skin, making it stronger and more resilient over time. The tea can also help to remove dead skin cells, revealing smoother, healthier skin underneath.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for cracked heels because they help to soften and exfoliate the skin, removing dead skin cells and promoting healthy skin regeneration.

Aloin is good for cracked heels because it helps to dry out and heal the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further irritation, making it a useful natural remedy for soothing and repairing dry, cracked skin.
Flavonoids help to repair and soften cracked heels by reducing inflammation, promoting blood flow, and protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for cracked heels
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea to help with cracked heels, start by gathering dried Althaea officinalis root or marshmallow root, which is available at most health food stores or online. Rinse the dried root with cold water, then place one to two teaspoons of the root in a cup. Pour in boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into another cup, making sure to squeeze the root to get all the goodness out. You can add honey to sweeten the tea, if desired. For cracked heels, drink two to three cups of this tea per day. The Althaea officinalis root is rich in mucilage, a soothing gel-like substance that helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin, making it a great natural remedy to help heal and soften dry, cracked heels.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is good for cracked heels because it has healing properties that help to fix the cracks. The tea is made from the juice of the aloe vera plant, which is rich in vitamins and minerals. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the heels. Aloe vera also has moisturizing properties that help to keep the skin hydrated and soft. When you drink aloe vera tea, the nutrients are absorbed into your body and help to repair damaged skin cells. This can help to heal cracked heels from the inside out. The tea also helps to soften and smooth out the skin, reducing the appearance of cracks and dryness. Regularly drinking aloe vera tea can help to keep your heels healthy and looking their best.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal teas for cracked heels
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal teas that help with cracked heels, start by gathering clean and dry Althaea officinalis roots. You can purchase these roots from a health food store or an online herbal supplier. Next, wash the roots under cold running water to remove any dirt or impurities. Then, chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150°F (65°C) for 1-2 hours, or until they are completely dry and crumbly. Once the roots are dry, you can store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months. To make the tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of the dried roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids. Let the tea cool before applying it to your cracked heels using a cotton pad or a soaked cotton ball. The soothing properties of Althaea officinalis can help to soften and moisturize your skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, made from licorice root, can be good for cracked heels. This is because the tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the heels. The tea also has moisturizing properties that help to soften and hydrate the skin on the heels, making it less prone to cracking. The antioxidants in the tea can also help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking. Additionally, the tea has antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infections in cracked heels, which can be painful and take time to heal. Drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea can also help to improve blood circulation, which can help to promote healthy skin cell growth and repair of damaged skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for cracked heels because they help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which soothes and softens dry, cracked skin on the heels.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for cracked heels
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea that helps with cracked heels, start by gathering the required ingredients. You'll need dried roots or leaves of the marshmallow plant, also known as Althaea officinalis. If you have access to fresh roots or leaves, dry them first by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once you have your dried ingredients, place one teaspoon of the dried roots or leaves in a cup. Pour in boiling water, and let it steep for about 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add a bit of honey or sugar to taste, but be careful not to add too much as it can make the tea too sweet. Let the tea cool down before applying it to your cracked heels. Soak your feet in the tea for about 10-15 minutes, then dry them thoroughly and apply a moisturizer to lock in the moisture. Repeat this process a few times a week for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tea is a natural remedy that can help to heal and soften cracked heels. The oats used to make this tea are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that help to nourish and moisturize dry skin. The tea also contains a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin. When applied topically, the tea can help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further drying out. This can be especially helpful during the dry winter months when the skin tends to become drier and more brittle. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can help to reduce swelling and redness associated with cracked heels, allowing the skin to heal faster. Drinking the tea internally can also help to improve circulation and promote healthy skin from the inside out, which can help to prevent cracked heels from forming in the first place.

Saponins are good for cracked heels because they help to soften and moisturize dry skin, reducing irritation and inflammation, and promoting healthy skin cell growth.

Phenolic acids are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the skin, making them effective in soothing and repairing dry, cracked skin on the heels.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea for cracked heels, start by rinsing 1-2 tablespoons of dried Althaea officinalis root in cold water. Then, boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the dried root in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds. Strain the liquid into another cup, discarding the solids, and add honey or sugar to taste if desired. Althaea officinalis root contains mucilages that can help soothe and moisturize dry skin, making it an excellent remedy for cracked heels. To use the tea, soak your feet in it for 15-20 minutes, then gently scrub and moisturize your heels as usual. You can also drink the tea to reap the benefits of Althaea officinalis from the inside out, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a great natural remedy for cracked heels. This is because it has many healing properties that help to soften and moisturize dry skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, especially vitamin E and potassium, which are essential for keeping skin healthy. When you drink nettle tea, the antioxidants help to protect your skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness and cracking. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The potassium in nettle tea also helps to lock in moisture, keeping your skin hydrated and soft. As a result, the cracked skin on your heels starts to heal, and new skin cells begin to grow, filling in the cracks and leaving your skin smooth and healthy.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea that helps with cracked heels, start by gathering the dried roots of the marshmallow plant, also known as Althaea officinalis. Rinse the dried roots in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, place one to two teaspoons of the dried roots in a small pot or teapot. Add eight ounces of boiling water over the roots and let it steep for five to ten minutes. As the roots steep, the water will turn into a soothing and slightly sweet tea. Once the steeping time is complete, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots. Discard the roots and let the tea cool slightly before applying it to the cracked heels. Soak a cloth in the tea and apply it to the heels for about 20 minutes to help soften and heal the cracks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata tea, also known as Plantain tea, is a natural remedy that can help to soften and heal cracked heels. The tea is made from the leaves of the Plantago lanceolata plant, which is rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. These compounds can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with cracked heels. The antioxidants in the tea can also help to promote skin regeneration, speeding up the healing process and reducing the appearance of cracks and dry skin. Additionally, Plantago lanceolata tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels. When applied topically, the tea can help to lock in moisture and protect the skin, promoting a healthy and hydrated appearance. Drinking the tea regularly can also help to improve skin health from the inside out, promoting overall skin health and well-being.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea that helps with cracked heels, you will need dried marshmallow root, a saucepan, a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and a cup. Start by boiling water in your saucepan. While the water is boiling, add one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root to your tea infuser or cheesecloth. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the marshmallow root steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. This will help release the root's medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time has passed, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, carefully pour the tea into your cup, straining the liquid if you used a tea infuser or cheesecloth. Drink the tea while it's still warm to help soften and soothe your cracked heels. You can drink one to two cups of Althaea officinalis tea per day to get the best results.

When applied topically, comfrey tea can help soften and moisturize the skin, making it easier to heal cracks and fissures. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also reduce redness and swelling, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels. Additionally, comfrey tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from further damage and promote collagen production, leading to healthier and more resilient skin. Regular use of comfrey tea can help to lock in moisture, reduce the appearance of cracks, and promote a smoother, more even skin texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis tea, also known as marshmallow root tea, has been used for centuries to heal dry and cracked skin. The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a thick and gooey substance that acts as a protective barrier on the skin. When applied topically or ingested as a tea, the mucilage helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, making it an excellent remedy for cracked heels. The anti-inflammatory properties of Althaea officinalis tea also reduce swelling and pain associated with dry skin, allowing the skin to heal faster. Additionally, the tea is rich in antioxidants that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin regeneration. Drinking Althaea officinalis tea helps to hydrate the body from the inside out, making it easier for the skin to retain moisture and repair itself.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea that helps with cracked heels, start by gathering dried marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis) from a trusted supplier. Begin by adding one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root to a cup of boiling water. Remove from heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the root to release its medicinal properties into the water. Once the tea has cooled slightly, strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids. Add honey to taste if desired, as the sweetness can help soothe dry skin. Drink the tea 2-3 times a week to help soften and heal cracked heels. After soaking, gently scrub the heels with a pumice stone and apply a rich moisturizer to lock in moisture and promote healing.

supplementary_best: To remedy cracked heels, a combination of herbal teas is more effective than a single herb. The best combination includes 'Aloe barbadensis', 'Aloe vera', and 'Symphytum officinale'. 'Aloe barbadensis' and 'Aloe vera' provide moisturizing and soothing properties to the skin, helping to soften and repair dry, cracked skin. 'Symphytum officinale' or 'Comfrey' root, meanwhile, is known for its ability to promote skin cell growth and tissue repair. This herb helps to rebuild damaged skin tissue and promote faster healing.  When brewed together, these three herbs create a soothing and moisturizing tea that can be applied topically to cracked heels. Soak your feet in warm water infused with this herbal tea mixture for 15-20 minutes, then gently scrub and moisturize your heels. This treatment can be repeated daily for faster results.
supplementary_avoid: If you have cracked heels, there are some herbal teas you should avoid or drink with caution. Tea made from Slippery Elm (Ulmus rubra) is not recommended as it can dry out the skin and make dryness worse. This is because it has astringent properties that can strip the skin of its natural moisture. Similarly, tea made from Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana) should be avoided as it can further dry out the skin, making the cracked heels worse. Yarrow tea (Achillea millefolium) should also be used with care as it can cause skin irritation in some people, which can exacerbate cracked heels. Chamomile tea (Matricaria chamomilla) is a gentle herb, but it can cause skin sensitivity in some individuals, which can make the cracked heels more sensitive. Finally, tea made from Dandelion root (Taraxacum officinale) is not recommended as it can increase urine production, leading to dehydration if not balanced with enough water intake, and dry skin.
supplementary_other_remedies: For cracked heels, some people use pumice stones to gently remove dead skin. This helps to smooth out the rough patches. Baking soda can also be used as a scrub to remove dry skin, and it has antibacterial properties to prevent infections. Oatmeal, when mixed with warm water, creates a paste that can be applied to the heels to soften and moisturize the skin. Coconut oil, rich in fatty acids, can be applied to the heels to lock in moisture and help repair the skin. Epsom salt baths, containing magnesium, can help to soften the skin and reduce inflammation. Using a humidifier in the home can also help to keep the skin hydrated by adding moisture to the air.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with cracked heels are likely to experience other skin issues due to dry skin and poor circulation. Eczema, a condition that causes red, itchy, and inflamed skin, can develop on the heels and feet. This is because cracked heels often have open wounds, making the skin more susceptible to infections.  Aloe vera gel, rich in vitamins and minerals, can help soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  Fungal infections, like athlete's foot, can also affect people with cracked heels. This is because the cracked skin creates an ideal environment for fungi to grow. Tea tree oil, with its antifungal properties, can help combat fungal infections by killing the fungi and promoting a healthy environment for the skin to heal.  Dry skin on the heels can lead to calluses and hard skin, which can be painful and difficult to manage. Turmeric, with its anti-inflammatory properties, can help reduce pain and inflammation, while also promoting healthy skin cell growth.  Poor circulation can cause swelling in the feet and ankles, which can lead to conditions like varicose veins. Ginkgo biloba, a medicinal herb that improves blood circulation, can help reduce swelling and promote healthy blood flow, reducing the risk of varicose veins.  People with cracked heels are also prone to ingrown toenails, caused by poor foot hygiene and pressure on the toes. St. John's Wort, with its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, can help soothe and protect the skin around the toenails, reducing the risk of ingrown toenails.  Cracked heels can also lead to pain and discomfort when walking, making it difficult to perform daily activities. Willow bark, with its anti-inflammatory properties, can help reduce pain and inflammation, promoting comfort and mobility.
